params.name: Venus de Milo
params.description:
On Aug 19, 1820 AD, this statue was unearthed on Milos in the Aegean Sea.
When the statue was unearthed, both of her arms were missing. Luckily, her head and her body remained intact. Her cloth covered the waist, but you can still sneak a peek of her toes. She has a sassy feminine form. Many believe the sculpture represents the pinnacle of ancient Greek art. [Basic Info] Date of Creation: Hellenistic Period Material: Marble Type: Relief Art Sculptor: Alexandros Height: 2.04 meters Collected b …
